summary: Set 102-git-workflow-automation: final session (3 of 3). Session 3 re-cut docs/tutorials/module-team-hello-world.md (719 lines) to the automation-first flow the set shipped: the per-set loop (open PR -> approve/merge on the git host -> finalize -> tag) now runs through the five confirm-gated commands (Dabbler: Open PR for this set / Finalize merged set / Cut release tag / Start hotfix from tag / Roll back to tag), host-neutral across GitHub and Azure DevOps, with every human-decision point called out explicitly (PR review/approval, branch protection, what/when to release, rollback authorization) and every Expect block quoting the shipped dialog text verbatim. One-time bootstrap (Parts 1/3/7) stays hand-taught with GitHub as the worked example plus short Azure DevOps equivalent callouts (spec non-goal: bootstrap is policy, not toil). New Part 0.5 per-host setup section (install/auth/PAT alternative/settings/what-green-looks-like, mirroring the in-product hostCli.ts preflight guidance) and a new 'Git under the hood' appendix (the exact raw sequence each command runs, PR step for both hosts, plus what the automation deliberately does NOT cover: authoring/hotfix branch cleanup, one-time bootstrap, PR approval itself). Doc sweep: extension README (new 'What you get' headline bullet + Other-features entry), docs/quick-start.md, and the scaffolded getting-started.md.template now reference the commands; cold-start golden fixtures regenerated via UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 (tripwire diff exactly the new template paragraph, +10 lines per tier); the companion workflow review prompt needed no change (it audits host-agnostic repo end-state); screenshots: none affected (shipped PNGs render from HTML mockups of surfaces 102 never touched - recorded determination, not an omission). Release staging: extension CHANGELOG 0.45.0 section + package.json 0.44.0->0.45.0 (publish operator-gated, tag vsix-v0.45.0, queued behind 0.42.0/0.43.0/0.44.0; registry-live 0.40.0); dabbler-ai-router stays 0.33.0 with intentionally no 102 changelog entry (extension-only set, recorded in the 0.45.0 blockquote); package-lock.json stays 0.41.0 per existing convention. Authoring provenance per the routed step-3.5 analysis (s3-ai-assignment-analysis.json): the tutorial re-cut and changelog section were ROUTED documentation calls (gemini-2.5-pro, $0.13 + $0.01, truncation-clean), then orchestrator-fidelity-passed against the shipped code (~20 restorations/corrections: compressed teaching passages restored, quick-pick-only-when->1-worktree accuracy, 'Hotfix branch name' input title, annotated-not-signed tag, dirty-tree-warns-not-refuses on Open PR, ADO UAT armed-not-run). Suites green: extension unit 1767 passing / 0 failing (vscode-stub path; electron launcher known-broken on VS Code 1.128, standing environment issue), pytest 3030 passed / 6 skipped (identical baseline), tsc --noEmit clean, esbuild + template copy clean; eslint carries 7 pre-existing errors + 58 warnings in five test files untouched by Set 102 (recorded baseline hygiene item, not a 102 defect); Layer 3 Playwright deliberately not run per L-064-12 scoping (docs + version metadata only). Verification: phased discovery with an up-front conventions file (suite baseline, release contract, by-design exclusions, L-095-1 consequence rubric), verifier gpt-5-6 (anthropic auto-excluded), fan-out 2/2 -> VERIFIED with ZERO findings of any severity, $0.38, no supplementary/remediation phases needed. End-of-set: change-log.md authored; advisory path-aware critique run via the sanctioned pull surface (Set 096 precedent), artifact path-aware-critique.json (raw, never edited). Critique adjudication (L-070-1 per-finding record): [openai gpt-5.4, Major false-confidence 'path-aware-critique.json not on disk'] = MOOT BY CONSTRUCTION - a timing artifact of the critique run itself (the critic's read-only sandbox snapshot predates the artifact its own run wrote; the disposition referencing the artifact was authored minutes before the producer wrote it); the artifact exists and ships in this very close commit, which is exactly the fix the finding demands - no retraction needed. [google gemini-2.5-pro, Minor contract-drift 'tutorial says any-branch-that-isn't-the-trunk vs changelog's detached-HEAD refusal'] = FIXED in-session with a one-clause tutorial clarification ('you must be ON a branch: a detached HEAD is refused'); technically a detached HEAD is not a branch so the texts never contradicted, but the clarification is cheap and strictly better. Both adjudications converge the critique per L-070-1 (no new code defect; one wording improvement). Deferred/follow-ons (operator-facing): (1) the armed Azure DevOps live UAT walk (needs a scratch ADO project, operator-assisted, armed since Session 1); (2) a GitHub-Copilot-flavored cut of the hello-world tutorial - operator directive received mid-session 2026-07-14 ('my staff uses Copilot') - to be authored as its own follow-on set, pairing naturally with the ADO walk; (3) eslint pre-existing test-file baseline cleanup candidate; (4) publish at the operator's click. Next-set recommendation (routed): pause for the operator-gated publish first, then 077-redo once the release is confirmed stable.
